_meta.name: PadelMatch Shot Taxonomy
_meta.status: IN PROGRESS — defense band, right side. Other cells stubbed.
_meta.purpose: Canonical, position-keyed map of padel shot vocabulary. Serves three jobs: (1) the fine-grained strokes self-assessment (player paints a comfort map onto it), (2) need-mapping for the Grow learning library (videos tag to shot nodes), (3) optional self-paced exploration surface ('what's my vocabulary from here?').
_meta.model: Position-primary spatial grammar. 'Each position on the court has its own vocabulary.' Full-court comfort = fluency.
key_decisions: Position is the PRIMARY axis (not difficulty).
A player occupies ONE side per match — left and right are two INDEPENDENT vocabularies, not mirror images.
Shots are defined ONCE and MAPPED to the cells they're played from (not duplicated per cell).
NO objective difficulty tier. Instead each shot carries a PER-PLAYER self-rated COMFORT score 0-6.
Comfort is per-player data (an overlay), NOT a property of the shot. Taxonomy ships once; each player paints their own comfort map.
'Off-the-glass' is NOT a family — it's a CONTACT variant axis (most defensive shots can be hit direct OR off the glass).
Variants (contact / direction / spin) are OPTIONAL depth, not counted as separate canonical nodes.

comfort_scale._note: Per-player, self-assessed, PRIVATE. Behavioral anchors resist inflation. 0 is an AWARENESS gap (distinct learning intervention) vs 1+ which is a COMPETENCE gap.

_sides_note: Built independently. Right side built first; left side is mostly re-mapping the same canonical shots to its cells (vocabulary overlaps; cell-mapping and tactical signature differ). FH/BH assignment flips by side.

vertical_bands._note: Net → baseline, named by TACTICAL ROLE (not by the shot played there), so a gap reads as a tactical self-diagnosis.
vertical_bands.net: At the net, attacking. Put-away vocabulary (x4, finishing volleys, drops).
vertical_bands.attack-control: Just behind the net, still offensive. Vibora/x3, aggressive volleys.
vertical_bands.recovery-transition: No-man's-land. Scrambling OUT of defense back toward the net. Bandeja (holding overhead) lives here. The zone amateurs are weakest in.
vertical_bands.defense: Baseline / back position. Returning, or pinned while opponents are at the net. Groundstrokes, lobs, glass recovery, the bajada (offensive exit).

horizontal_bands._note: From measured court thirds (1.66m each). Per side.
horizontal_bands.side: Against the side wall. With 'defense' band = the back corner (double-wall vocabulary).
horizontal_bands.middle: Central to the player's own half. The 'home' position for the band.
horizontal_bands.T: Nearest the centre service line / the T.

_families_note: 'off-the-glass' removed as a family — folded into the contact variant axis.

variant_axes._note: Optional depth. A fully-specified shot picks one value per applicable axis. Not all axes apply to all shots (e.g. bajada is off-glass by definition). Canonical node count ignores variants.

shots.name: Forehand bajada
shots._note: The only OFFENSIVE off-the-glass shot from the back. Bridges defense->attack. Off-glass by definition.

defense-middle.tactical_note: The 'home' defensive position — where you stand returning when opponents are at the net. Richest foundational cell.
open_questions: Is a DEFENSIVE BLOCK / RESET shot (absorb pace, drop short when jammed) missing here, or does it live in another cell/band?

defense-side.tactical_note: The back corner. Holds the brutal DOUBLE-WALL recovery vocabulary (contact: off-side-glass, double-wall). Hardest cell in the band.

left._status: NOT STARTED — build after right side complete.

per_player_overlay_schema._note: NOT part of this shared file. Documents how per-player comfort is stored separately.
